resume:
Operations Intern Candidate

Education: BS Industrial Engineering, junior year, Purdue University. GPA 3.6.

Experience:
Delmar Foods — Operations Intern (last summer)
- Supported the warehouse ops team on a slotting optimization project.
- Led the project end to end and presented results to the plant manager.
- Elsewhere on resume: assisted a senior analyst who owned the slotting project; I gathered the data.
- Built a few Excel dashboards tracking pick rates.

Campus: Treasurer of the Supply Chain Club; helped organize a plant tour for 30 students.

Skills: Excel, basic SQL, process mapping, time studies, Lean fundamentals (green belt in progress).

Note on availability: available full time after graduation next year; also states available to start immediately this summer.
jd: Delmar Foods is hiring an Operations Intern to support our distribution-center operations team for a 12-week summer program. You will work alongside analysts and supervisors on real throughput and labor-efficiency projects: time studies, slotting and pick-path analysis, and dashboard reporting on pick rates and order cycle time. We value interns who can speak precisely and consistently about what they personally did versus what the team did, and who answer an interviewer's question directly rather than turning it back. You will report to a senior operations analyst and present your project findings to site leadership at the end of the program. Strong candidates are comfortable with data, curious about how physical operations work, and honest about the limits of their own contribution.

history.q: Tell me about the slotting project at Delmar — what was it and what did you do on it?
Your resume says you 'led it end to end' in one spot and 'assisted a senior analyst who owned it' in another. Which is accurate?
Got it. For the part you actually did — the data gathering — walk me through your method and what you found.

candidate_last_answer: Well, can I ask you something first — how does your team usually define 'owning' a project for an intern? Because I feel like that word means different things at different companies. And honestly, wouldn't you say that for an intern, gathering and structuring all the data is basically leading the analytical part? What would you have called it in my position?

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: wouldn't you say that for an intern, gathering and structuring all the data is basically leading

expected-yield: decline to relitigate the definition and pin the candidate to a factual account of their own data-gathering method and what they specifically found, resolving the lead-versus-assist contradiction with concrete actions
